findings.description: The infographic layout with three rounded pill-shaped sections (Guidelines, Printing, Before Sending) arranged around a central circle is completely lost. The HTML renders it as a flat, linear text layout.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a visually structured infographic with three vertical pill/oval sections arranged side by side with a central circle. HTML shows a simple image followed by plain text in a single column.

findings.description: The infographic is included as a full image in the HTML, and then the text content is also extracted below it, creating duplication.
findings.evidence: PDF has one infographic. HTML shows the infographic image AND all its text content repeated below as plain text.

findings.description: The OCR/text extraction below the infographic image contains garbled/corrupted text artifacts.
findings.evidence: HTML shows strings like ',1 t*l', '..............', '•.=====.=====m=u;', '::::•', '·=!!!!::!!•', 'c; ;1', '..….(8)' which are not present in the original PDF.

findings.description: The GAS-1201ME form layout with side-by-side fields is collapsed into a single-column list in the HTML. The original has a complex form layout with fields arranged in multiple columns.
findings.evidence: PDF shows fields like Street Address and County side by side, City/State/Zip in a row, and 'Fill in applicable circles' box on the right. HTML lists all fields vertically in a single column.

findings.description: Part 1 Gallonage Accountability is rendered as a proper table in HTML, which reasonably represents the form structure. Part 2 is also rendered as a table. These are acceptable conversions.
findings.evidence: PDF shows numbered lines with fill-in blanks; HTML shows structured tables with Line Number, Description, and value columns.

findings.description: The NCDOR logo at the top of the GAS-1201ME form is missing from the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NCDOR logo in the top-left corner of the form. HTML has no logo image.

findings.description: The form title 'GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment' is reduced to just a heading at the bottom of page 1 text, losing its prominent positioning as the form header.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'GAS-1201ME' prominently at the top of the form page. In the HTML, the form title appears as a heading 'NCDOR GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment' at the transition between pages.

findings.description: The 'Fill in applicable circles' section and FEIN/SSN and Refund for Quarter Ending sections were in the right column of the form but are now placed sequentially after all the left-column fields, changing the reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF has these sections positioned to the right of the name/address fields. HTML places them after all address fields in a linear sequence.

findings.description: Page 1 content is duplicated - appears both as an image with alt text and then again as fully rendered text sections (Guidelines, Printing, Before Sending). The infographic content from page 1 is reproduced twice in the HTML.
findings.evidence: The image has alt text describing the infographic, then the same content appears again as structured sections with headings 'Guidelines', 'Printing', 'Before Sending' with all the same text repeated.

findings.description: The 'Before Sending' section has garbled text. The PDF says 'Do not submit photocopies of returns' but the HTML says 'ocopies of returns.' - missing the first part of the sentence.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Do not submit photocopies of returns. Submit originals only.' HTML: 'ocopies of returns. Submit originals only.'

findings.description: Multiple h1 elements exist in the HTML. There is an h1 for 'Instructions For Handwritten Forms' and another h1 for 'NCDOR GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment'. Additionally, nested <main> tags are used improperly.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains:

Instructions For Handwritten Forms

and

NCDOR GAS-1201ME Motor Fuels Claim for Refund Special Mobile Equipment

findings.description: OCR artifacts and garbled text from the infographic page are included as literal text in the HTML, which are not meaningful content.
findings.evidence: HTML includes: ',1 t®I', '•·====-====m=u:', '::::••', '··=!!!:::!:•', '·-· -', 'c; ;1', ',,___(8)' — these are OCR noise from the infographic, not actual document content.

findings.description: The NCDOR header text is garbled in the HTML rendering of page 1.
findings.evidence: HTML: 'CAROLINA NC • DEPARTMENT D(i) R I OF REVENUE NORTH' — should represent the NCDOR logo/header for North Carolina Department of Revenue.
